questions.question: How should these three features be packaged?

options.label: One combined plan (Recommended)
options.description: All three in a single plan. Features 1 & 2 share the new non-terminal pane infrastructure; feature 3 is a small add-on. Matches the repo's single-feature-with-followups style.

options.label: Two plans
options.description: One plan for the viewer panes (HTML/MD + diff review, shared infra), a separate tiny plan for the {timestamp} placeholder.

options.label: Three separate plans
options.description: Fully independent plans for each feature. More overhead but each executes/reverts in isolation.

questions.question: How should opening an HTML file render it? (Markdown is always marked + DOMPurify sanitized.)

options.label: Sandboxed iframe, no scripts (Recommended)
options.description: Render the HTML in an <iframe sandbox srcdoc> with scripts disabled. Safe by default; shows layout/styling/images but inert JS. Best for build reports, docs, design files.

options.label: Sanitized static HTML
options.description: Run the HTML through DOMPurify and inject into a div. Strips scripts AND iframes/forms; loses some layout fidelity. Consistent with the markdown path.

options.label: Sandboxed iframe, scripts allowed
options.description: allow-scripts in the sandbox so interactive HTML (charts, etc.) works. More capable but weaker isolation — a malicious file could run JS in the sandboxed frame.

questions.question: How should the worktree diff / code review be rendered?

options.label: Hand-rolled CSS unified diff (Recommended)
options.description: Parse git diff output and style it (red/green lines, file headers) with our own CSS. No new dependency — keeps the repo's minimal-deps ethos (only xterm/react today).

options.label: diff2html library
options.description: Adds the diff2html npm dep for GitHub-style side-by-side/unified rendering. Nicer UX out of the box, but a new dependency + its CSS bundled under the strict CSP.

options.label: Normal (Recommended)
options.description: Implement + unit tests for core logic (diff parsing, timestamp resolve, markdown sanitize) + e2e probe. Matches the repo's existing workflow.

options.label: TDD
options.description: Red-green-refactor cycles, tests written first for each unit. More rigorous; slower.
